_comment: macOS sudo opens /etc/sudoers with O_WRONLY (no O_TRUNC/O_APPEND/O_CREAT) so it can take an LOCK_EX flock for serialised reads. Flags=5 = O_WRONLY|O_NONBLOCK. The rule must treat this as a non-tamper because the fd is never written to (sudo only locks-then-reads). Without this guard every `sudo` invocation on the host fires a false-positive alert (observed: 30 hits in 15 min during rc.6 QA on edr-qa).
